steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for a choice that refutes a scientific assumption. The assumption: both formation and collapse temperatures vary with molecular variations. The key finding from the 2023 study that does the refuting: collapse temperature was "nearly independent of material and fluid properties, contrary to previous theories."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Contrary to the hypothesis that vapor layer formation temperature is dictated by molecular variation, a 2023 study confirmed that the temperature at which the vapor layer forms depends on the surface and liquid used.': The formation temperature confirmed the hypothesis — it does vary with molecular properties. Calling this a refutation misreads the data. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Though the vapor layer created by the Leidenfrost effect can be formed at a range of temperatures, the temperature at which the layer collapses varies depending on material and fluid properties.': This restates the original assumption as true — exactly the opposite of what the study found. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Countering previous hypotheses, a recent study found that Leidenfrost vapor's collapse temperature does not vary depending on material and fluid properties.': Directly states the collapse temperature is independent of material and fluid properties, and explicitly frames this as countering previous hypotheses. Matches the refutation perfectly. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Scientists had hypothesized that the formation temperature of Leidenfrost vapor depends on variations in surfaces and liquids used, but a 2023 study confirmed that the collapse temperature remains consistent.': Noting the collapse temperature is consistent is true, but the choice frames the hypothesis as being about formation temperature only — not the collapse temperature — so no actual refutation of the hypothesis occurs. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
